title: erigeron uniflorus subsp. parnassensis
intro: Erigeron uniflorus subsp. parnassensis, also known as the dwarf fleabane, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
